source_quote: Wachter v. Gratech Co., Ltd., 2000 ND 62, ¶ 22, 608 N.W.2d 279.
evidence: DB text contains 'Wachter v. Gratech Co.,Ltd.' (missing space after comma); authoritative PDF source shows 'Wachter v. Gratech Co., Ltd.' with proper spacing in the citation. This is a standard citation formatting error (OCR or ingestion defect).
note: Single occurrence in both DB and source. The space after 'Co.,' is required by Bluebook citation format and is present in the official PDF.

source_quote: expenses of these disciplinary proceedings in the amount of $250.
evidence: PDF source (pdftotext) shows '$250' without space before the dollar sign. DB text has 'amount of $ 250.' appearing exactly once. Source PDF text clearly shows 'amount of $250.' in paragraph 7 context.

source_quote: expenses of these disciplinary proceedings in the amount of $250 within 90 days of entry of the judgment, payable to the Secretary of the Disciplinary
evidence: PDF source shows '$250 within' without space. DB text has 'amount of $ 250 within' appearing exactly once. Both occurrences of the dollar amount exhibit the same spacing error—OCR artifact from ingestion.

evidence: Extra leading space on wrapped line in DB: 0x0a 0x20 (newline + space) vs PDF: 0x0a (newline only). Source shows no space between line break and 'd' of 'disregarded'.

source_quote:
35.1(a)(2), (4), and (7); see State v. Kensmoe,
2001 ND 190, ¶ 17, 636 N.W.2d 183 ("[A] question not raised or considered in the
trial court cannot be
evidence: The DB text contains an extra space before the semicolon after '(7)'. The authoritative PDF source shows the correct form 'and (7);' without the space, consistent with standard punctuation practice. This is a mechanical transcription/ingestion error.

The authoritative PDF source shows '§ 38-09-19:' (no space before colon) while the DB text shows '§ 38-09-19 :' (space before colon). Comprehensive scan of the PDF reveals zero instances of space-before-colon pattern and six instances of the no-space pattern, confirming this is the standard format for the opinion.
